step_by_step_thinking: I agree that bullet fingerprinting refers to primary markings, but it’s important to note that while they do provide valuable information about who may have touched the gun prior to discharge, these marks don't necessarily indicate post-discharge alterations of the bullet itself. The term specifically relates to pre-firing contact traces found primarily on the exterior surfaces like the barrel or grip rather than any changes to the bullet after firing.
